reasoning: Without real-time search capabilities to observe data between October 30 and November 9, 2025, the most rational approach is to anchor to the most recent efficient market consensus. The market price on October 30 was 0.58 for 'no change' at the March 2026 meeting. Market prices 4-5 months out typically capture the distribution of expected policy paths accurately, reflecting a base case of a pause or hold regime but pricing in moderate odds of an inflation/growth surprise prompting a rate adjustment. I adopt the market's 58% probability.
